content.text:
Probability measures the likelihood of events in situations involving 
uncertainty.

content.title: Sample Space and Events
content.content:
The **sample space** $S$ is the set of all 
possible outcomes of an experiment.

An **event** $E$ is a subset of $S$. An event 
**occurs** if the outcome is in $E$.

content.title: Dice
content.problem:
Roll a die. $S = \{1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6\}$.

$E = \{2, 4, 6\}$ is the event "roll an even number."

content.title: Laplace's Definition
content.content:
If all outcomes in finite sample space $S$ are equally likely, then:
\[ P(E) = \frac{|E|}{|S|} = \frac{\text{favorable outcomes}}{\text{total outcomes}} \]

content.title: Card probability
content.problem:
A card is drawn from a standard 52-card deck. What is the probability of 
drawing a heart?

content.title: Kolmogorov Axioms
content.content:
For any event $E$ in sample space $S$:

1. $0 \leq P(E) \leq 1$

2. $P(S) = 1$

3. If $E_1, E_2, \ldots$ are mutually exclusive, then 
 $P(E_1 \cup E_2 \cup \cdots) = P(E_1) + P(E_2) + \cdots$
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Basic Probability Rules
content.content:
1. $P(\emptyset) = 0$

2. $P(\bar{E}) = 1 - P(E)$ (complement rule)

3. $P(E \cup F) = P(E) + P(F) - P(E \cap F)$ (inclusion-exclusion)

4. If $E \subseteq F$, then $P(E) \leq P(F)$
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Complement
content.problem: When rolling two dice, what is the probability that the sum is at least 4?
content.solution:
Easier to compute the complement. Sums less than 4: (1,1)=2, (1,2)=3, (2,1)=3.

$P(\text{sum} < 4) = 3/36 = 1/12$.

$P(\text{sum} \geq 4) = 1 - 1/12 = 11/12$.

content.title: Inclusion-exclusion
content.problem: A card is drawn. What is $P(\text{heart or face card})$?

marginNotes.text: Time and chance happen to them all.
